name: Pimienta Alimentos Backend
description: Production Spring Boot API for Pimienta Alimentos — a real-world company operations platform used daily by employees and staff. Covers HR, payroll, inventory, CRM, contracts, tasks, and file management.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Fragmented operations for a growing food company
problemStatement.problemDescription: Pimienta Alimentos needed a single backend to coordinate employees, payroll, inventory, sales opportunities, contracts, and internal tasks across headquarters — replacing spreadsheets, ad-hoc tools, and disconnected processes that did not scale with daily staff operations.
problemList: Employee records, attendance, and work schedules lived in separate spreadsheets with no audit trail
Inventory movements (purchase, sale, transfer) lacked approval workflows and real-time stock visibility
Payroll periods and adjustments were error-prone without a centralized ledger
CRM opportunities and internal projects had no shared pipeline or milestone tracking
No secure, role-based API for the company frontend used by staff in the field and office
Bulk data updates (headquarters, tasks, payroll) required manual re-entry

solutionList.title: Modular bounded contexts
solutionList.description: Ten domain modules (account, employees, contract, CRM, task, headquarter, inventory, payroll, files, notification) each follow ports-and-adapters architecture.

solutionList.title: JWT authentication with admin approval
solutionList.description: Staff register and log in via JWT; new accounts require administrator approval before access. Refresh tokens stored in Redis (Upstash in production).

solutionList.title: Operational workflows
solutionList.description: Inventory transactions, contract renewals, opportunity pipeline (win/lose/reopen), attendance start/end workday, and payroll adjustments are first-class API workflows.

solutionList.title: Bulk XLSX import/export
solutionList.description: Apache POI powers spreadsheet sync for employees, opportunities, tasks, headquarters, and payroll — matching how operations teams already work.

solutionList.title: AWS-backed file storage
solutionList.description: Employee photos and file assets upload to S3 with presigned download URLs and server-side image resizing.

solutionList.title: Production-ready infrastructure
solutionList.description: Deployed on AWS EC2 with RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis; Docker images built via multi-stage Maven pipeline.

mediaGallery.title: System screenshots
mediaGallery.description: Placeholder gallery — add frontend screenshots or architecture diagrams when available.

metrics.label: Active users
metrics.description: Used daily by Pimienta Alimentos employees and administrators in production.

metrics.label: Deployment
metrics.description: Spring Boot JAR in Docker on EC2; RDS PostgreSQL; Upstash Redis.

metrics.label: OpenAPI
metrics.description: Interactive API reference with Bearer JWT security scheme.

layers.name: Inbound adapters (Web)
layers.description: REST controllers, DTOs, Jakarta validation, and per-endpoint OpenAPI Doc* annotations.
responsibilities: Role checks via Spring Security + @PreAuthorize where needed

layers.name: Application (use cases)
layers.description: Orchestrates domain logic via input ports; commands and queries per bounded context.
responsibilities: Transaction boundaries and domain event publication
Mapping between commands and domain operations
Coordination across output ports (repos, S3, email)

layers.name: Domain
layers.description: Entities, value objects, domain exceptions, and business rules per module.
components: BaseDomain<ID> with audit fields and soft delete

layers.name: Outbound adapters
layers.description: JPA repositories, S3 storage, Redis token store, email senders.
components: JPA entities and Spring Data repositories

layers.name: Cross-cutting config
layers.description: Security, CORS, rate limiting, OpenAPI, scheduling, audit logging.

designPatterns.title: Hexagonal architecture
designPatterns.description: Each module splits core (domain + application + ports) from adapters (web, persistence, external).

designPatterns.title: Ports and adapters
designPatterns.description: Input ports (use case interfaces) and output ports (repository, storage) keep domain free of framework code.

designPatterns.title: Repository pattern
designPatterns.description: JPA-backed repositories implement output ports; aggregates extend BaseDomain.

designPatterns.title: Domain events
designPatterns.description: e.g. AccountPendingApprovalEvent triggers notification listeners asynchronously.

designPatterns.title: Token bucket rate limiting
designPatterns.description: Global Redis filter plus per-controller @RateLimit profiles (STRICT, READ_HEAVY, etc.).

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API instances
scalabilityStrategies.description: JWT + Redis refresh tokens allow horizontal scaling of EC2/Docker replicas behind a load balancer (placeholder: ALB not yet documented in repo).

scalabilityStrategies.title: Managed RDS and Upstash
scalabilityStrategies.description: Database and cache are external to the container; API container is lightweight and replaceable.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Key-prefixed Redis
scalabilityStrategies.description: PIMIENTA_REDIS_KEY_PREFIX avoids collisions on shared Upstash instances.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Pagination defaults
scalabilityStrategies.description: PageableRequest caps page size at 100; PagedResponse standardizes list endpoints.

securityStrategies.title: JWT Bearer authentication
securityStrategies.description: 15-minute access tokens; 7-day refresh tokens stored in Redis; logout invalidates refresh.

securityStrategies.title: Role-based access
securityStrategies.description: ROLE_ADMIN and ROLE_MANAGER enforced in SecurityConfig and method security.

securityStrategies.title: Admin-approved registration
securityStrategies.description: New users start PENDING_APPROVAL until an administrator approves the account.

securityStrategies.title: Rate limiting
securityStrategies.description: Per-IP global bucket on /api/** plus stricter profiles on auth and sensitive operations.

securityStrategies.title: Production actuator lockdown
securityStrategies.description: application-prod.yaml exposes only health and info; metrics require ADMIN.

cacheStrategies.name: Refresh token store
cacheStrategies.description: Redis keys for JWT refresh sessions; invalidated on logout.

cacheStrategies.name: Global rate limit buckets
cacheStrategies.description: Per-IP token bucket via Lua script (ratelimit-token-bucket.lua).

architectureFeatures.title: Flyway migrations
architectureFeatures.description: 12 versioned SQL migrations (V1 headquarters through V12 dummy data); ddl-auto validate.

architectureFeatures.title: OpenAPI per endpoint
architectureFeatures.description: Doc* annotation classes keep controllers thin and documentation consistent.

architectureFeatures.title: XLSX bulk operations
architectureFeatures.description: Import/export for employees, CRM, tasks, headquarters, payroll via Apache POI.

architectureFeatures.title: Audit logging
architectureFeatures.description: AuditLogInterceptor records operational actions for compliance traceability.

requestFlow.title: Client request
requestFlow.description: Staff app sends HTTP request with Authorization Bearer JWT to EC2-hosted API.

requestFlow.title: Security filter
requestFlow.description: JwtAuthenticationFilter validates token; GlobalRateLimitFilter checks Redis bucket.

requestFlow.title: Controller → use case
requestFlow.description: REST adapter maps DTO to command; application service executes domain logic.

requestFlow.title: Persistence / external
requestFlow.description: JPA writes to RDS; optional S3 upload or Redis session update.

requestFlow.title: PagedResponse / JSON
requestFlow.description: Standard pagination or entity DTO returned with OpenAPI-documented shape.

eventFlow.title: Listener
eventFlow.description: Notification module listener picks up event.

decisions.title: Hexagonal modules over layered monolith
decisions.problem: Ten operational domains (HR, inventory, payroll, CRM) would tangle in a single package.
decisions.solution: Bounded contexts under module/* with core + adapter split and shared kernel (BaseDomain, pagination).
alternatives: Single-layered Spring package by technical concern
decisions.outcome: Clear ownership per domain; deployable monolith suitable for current team size and EC2 footprint.

decisions.title: Upstash Redis over in-container Redis
decisions.problem: Production on EC2 needs durable cache/session store without co-locating Redis on the same VM.
decisions.solution: REDIS_URL with rediss:// to Upstash; key prefix for multi-tenant safety.
decisions.outcome: Managed TLS Redis for refresh tokens and rate limits with minimal ops overhead.

decisions.title: Flyway + validate ddl-auto
decisions.problem: Schema drift between environments risks production data corruption.
decisions.solution: Versioned migrations in db/migration; Hibernate validates entity mapping only.
decisions.outcome: Reproducible schema across local Docker, CI, and RDS.

decisions.title: JWT stateless sessions
decisions.problem: Staff clients need mobile-friendly auth without server-side HTTP sessions.
decisions.solution: Short-lived access JWT + Redis-backed refresh tokens; stateless SecurityFilterChain.
decisions.outcome: Scalable auth model aligned with SPA/frontend clients.

codeExamples.title: Thin REST adapter with use-case port
codeExamples.description: AuthController delegates to AuthUseCases input port — no business logic in the web layer. OpenAPI and rate limits applied declaratively.

files.name: AuthController.java
files.explanation: Driving adapter: maps HTTP DTOs to commands and invokes the application port.

codeExamples.title: Stateless JWT security filter chain
codeExamples.description: Public paths for auth, health, and Swagger; role rules for admin/manager routes; JWT filter before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter.

files.name: SecurityConfig.java
files.explanation: Central security policy for the production API used by all staff clients.

codeExamples.title: Shared aggregate base with audit fields
codeExamples.description: All domain entities extend BaseDomain for id, timestamps, soft delete (deletedAt), and optimistic locking (version).

files.name: BaseDomain.java
files.explanation: Repository-style thin aggregates share persistence metadata without JPA in the domain package.

codeExamples.title: Infrastructure-wide Redis rate limiter
codeExamples.description: OncePerRequestFilter applies token-bucket limits per client IP on all /api/** paths except health and actuator.

files.name: GlobalRateLimitFilter.java
files.explanation: Protects the production API from abuse while staff use the system daily.

codeExamples.title: Consistent pagination response
codeExamples.description: PagedResponse record wraps Spring Data Page into items + metadata for every list endpoint.

files.name: PagedResponse.java

codeExamples.title: Production multi-stage Dockerfile
codeExamples.description: Maven build on JDK 25, runtime on JRE 25 Alpine with non-root spring user — deployed on AWS EC2.

files.name: Dockerfile
files.explanation: Image built from backend/ context; SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=docker in production.
files.content:
FROM maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-25-alpine AS build
RUN mvn -q -B -DskipTests package

FROM eclipse-temurin:25-jre-alpine AS runtime
USER spring:spring
EXPOSE 8080
ENV SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=docker
ENTRYPOINT ["sh", "-c", "exec java ${JAVA_OPTS:-} -jar /app/app.jar"]

features.title: JWT authentication & session management
features.description: Register, login, refresh, and logout with BCrypt passwords. Refresh tokens persisted in Upstash Redis; access tokens expire in 15 minutes.
highlights: POST /api/v1/auth/refresh — rotates session via Redis store

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: ROLE_ADMIN and ROLE_MANAGER gate sensitive routes (files, notifications, user management) while staff access operational endpoints.
highlights: Admin-only file management and notification management
Manager + Admin access to file resources and notification logs

features.title: Redis token-bucket rate limiting
features.description: Global per-IP protection on /api/** plus per-endpoint @RateLimit profiles (STRICT, AUTH_SESSION, READ_HEAVY, etc.).

features.title: Employee & HR management
features.description: Full employee lifecycle: CRUD, photos (S3), work schedules, attendance start/end workday, search and export.
highlights: Multipart employee registration with photo upload
XLSX import/export for bulk HR updates

features.title: Inventory & stock workflows
features.description: Items, storage locations (tree), stock levels, transactions (purchase/sale/transfer/adjustment) with approve/complete workflow.
highlights: Movement history by item or reference

features.title: Payroll management
features.description: Payroll periods, records, payments, debts, adjustments, and XLSX sync for finance operations.

features.title: CRM — opportunities & projects
features.description: Sales pipeline (win/lose/reopen), linked tasks, projects with activate/hold/complete, and milestones.

features.title: Employment contracts
features.description: Contract creation, renewal, extension, and lifecycle management tied to employees.

features.title: Task management
features.description: Tasks with checklist, assignment, status updates, and XLSX import/export for operations teams.

features.title: Headquarters / branches
features.description: Branch locations with statistics, import/export, and linkage to employees and inventory.

features.title: File assets (S3)
features.description: Admin and manager file uploads with presigned download URLs; image resize before storage.
highlights: Separate admin management vs manager resources APIs

features.title: Email notifications
features.description: Domain-event driven notifications (e.g. pending account approval); admin search and statistics.

features.title: Standard pagination contract
features.description: PageableRequest (page 0-based, size 1–100) and PagedResponse<T> with PageMetadata across list endpoints.

features.title: OpenAPI 3 + Swagger UI
features.description: springdoc-openapi with Bearer JWT scheme; per-endpoint Doc* annotations for maintainable API docs.

features.title: Flyway database migrations
features.description: 12 versioned SQL migrations; schema pimienta; Hibernate ddl-auto validate in all environments.
highlights: V1 headquarters through V12 dummy data

features.title: MockMvc integration tests
features.description: 14 integration test suites covering auth, employees, inventory, payroll, CRM, and more.
highlights: Rate limiting disabled in test profile

components.name: Company frontend
components.description: Web app used by Pimienta staff (Angular; CORS-configured origins).

components.name: Mobile / field staff
components.description: Authenticated clients calling /api/v1/* with JWT Bearer tokens.

components.name: Docker container
components.description: Multi-stage image: Maven 3.9 + JDK 25 build → JRE 25 Alpine runtime.

components.name: Spring Boot API
components.description: Profile docker; port 8080; Flyway + JPA validate on startup.

components.name: RDS PostgreSQL
components.description: POSTGRES_URL via SPRING_DATASOURCE_* env vars in cloud compose.

components.name: Upstash Redis
components.description: REDIS_URL=rediss://... for TLS; PIMIENTA_REDIS_KEY_PREFIX namespaces keys.

dockerFiles.description: Multi-stage build from backend project root; non-root user; exposes 8080.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-25-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /workspace
COPY pom.xml .
COPY src ./src
RUN mvn -q -B -DskipTests package

FROM eclipse-temurin:25-jre-alpine AS runtime
WORKDIR /app
RUN addgroup -S spring -g 1001 && adduser -S spring -u 1001 -G spring
COPY --from=build /workspace/target/*.jar /app/app.jar
RUN mkdir -p /app/logs && chown -R spring:spring /app
USER spring:spring
EXPOSE 8080
ENV SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=docker
ENTRYPOINT ["sh", "-c", "exec java ${JAVA_OPTS:-} -jar /app/app.jar"]

dockerFiles.description: Connects to external RDS and Upstash Redis via .env — used in production on EC2.

httpEndpoints.description: Public liveness endpoint used by monitoring and load balancers. Returns UP status and service name.
Creates account in PENDING_APPROVAL state until an administrator approves. Used by company staff onboarding.

example.message: Registration successful. Your account is pending approval by an administrator.

httpEndpoints.description: Issues JWT access and refresh tokens. Refresh token stored in Upstash Redis.
Rotates access token using valid refresh token from Redis store.
Invalidates refresh token in Redis. Optional body with refreshToken.
Returns authenticated staff member profile.
Paginated user list for administrators.
Administrator approves a PENDING_APPROVAL account so staff can log in.
Paginated employee search used by HR staff.
Downloads spreadsheet of employee records for operations.
Field staff clock-in with optional geo and photo evidence.
Employment contracts with pagination.

Sales pipeline opportunities with filters and pagination.
Transitions opportunity to won state in CRM pipeline.
Internal task management for operations teams.
Aggregate stats across branch locations.
Records inventory purchase; may require approval workflow.
Alerts for items below threshold — used by warehouse staff.
Payroll records for finance operations.
